events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll inspect the micron gauge image, read the two displayed values, then write the JSON result directly to `/app/answer.json`.

events.label: assistant
events.text: The image shows 353 microns at pump isolation and 4857 microns after 10 minutes. I’m treating that rapid rise as a failed standing vacuum decay test, consistent with a leak, trapped moisture/outgassing, or incomplete evacuation that needs correction before charging/returning to service.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "cat > /tmp/answer.json.tmp <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"tested/inspected\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 90,
  \"value\": 4857,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.93,
  \"event_time\": 10.0,
  \"rate\": 450.4,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Micron reading rose from 353 microns at pump isolation to 4857 microns after 10 minutes.\",
    \"Standing vacuum decay test failed; evacuation did not hold below acceptable vacuum level.\",
    \"Decay behavior indicates likely leak, trapped moisture/non-condensables, or defective brazed/service connection requiring correction before charging.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Do not charge or return the refrigeration circuit to service based on this evacuation result.\",
    \"Leak-check brazed joints, service ports, cores, hoses, valve packings, and isolation points; repair confirmed defects.\",
    \"Repeat proper evacuation with cores removed and verify a stable standing micron test to OEM specification before opening charge.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed 10-minute standing reading is 4857 microns after isolating at 353 microns, a rise of 4504 microns or about 450.4 microns per minute. That magnitude of decay fails a normal HVAC-R evacuation hold test and indicates the system is not dry and tight enough for service.\",
  \"references\": [\"EPA 608\", \"mfr specs\"]
}
EOF
mv /tmp/answer.json.tmp /app/answer.json"
